---
title: 'Miles of Travel'
date: '2013-01-17T05:43:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Call To Excellence'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '5767427880651414874'
blogger_filename: '/2013/01/it-begins.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Call To Excellence/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'STORY'
  - 'Miles'
---

The chill passed through him despite the thick lining of his jacket. Miles stood beneath the inadequate aluminum cover of the buss stop huddled against the glass Coors Light ad. Maybe he was doing his part to shield the passerby from the temptation to engage. Then again a bucket of cold beers wasn't exactly as tempting on a 20 degree evening. The thought amused him. Miles had nearly forgotten the lifestyles associated with such a temptation. He'd been a "good boy" for so many years now.<br />
<a href="http://3.bp.blogspot.com/-a74XDnpEXTQ/UPTs22ImJ5I/AAAAAAAAKQ4/ly1rIX5MJaY/s1600/Bus+Stop+at+Night.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: right; float: right; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-left: 1em;"><img border="0" height="177" src="http://3.bp.blogspot.com/-a74XDnpEXTQ/UPTs22ImJ5I/AAAAAAAAKQ4/ly1rIX5MJaY/s320/Bus+Stop+at+Night.jpg" width="320" /></a><br />
The buss pulled into sight from beyond the street lights down the road. It had just turned the corner, only three blocks to reach him. Only three red lights between a warm ride and his inefficient jacket. It was unusual for Anaheim to get this cold. It wasn't unheard of, but unusual. Most logical people would have taken the car, but Miles hadn't owned a car in 3 years. So the buss it is. He'd have to work a few extra shifts and get a better jacket, maybe a scarf too.<br />
<br />
Three long minutes later the buss opened it's doors and he stepped inside and swiped his frequent flier card. That's not what the buss line called it, but he figured he'd been working on his positive confession and maybe someday he'd repair his credit enough to get one of those frequent flier cards. He did find you can save a ton of money by buying the passes for the whole month up front rather than "as you go". In fact his work was participating in a new "green energy" program and he was able to buy his buss ticket at discount and pre-tax through payroll deduction. "Very exciting..." he thought to himself with a smirk.<br />
<br />
He held his breath as he passed Old Bart, sleeping in his chair, strapped down to the steal floor. Miles had been buss bound so long that the usual smells didn't hardly phase him anymore. Then again, there were people like Old Bart to remind you that there comes a limit to even Miles' capacity to get "used to it". When he was awake Old Bart was quite the company. He'd go on and on about what was wrong with the country, the president, and how he'd do it better if he was running things. It seems like the homeless often have strong opinions, he thought to himself.<br />
<br />
Miles glanced around to find an empty seat, at this hour there are plenty. He avoided the rest of the passengers and sat in the back for the hour ride home He opened his bible and tried to read,&nbsp;but the two girls in front were too loud for that, their hang over tomorrow was bound to make them regret tonight, if they remembered tonight at all, which he doubted they would by the way they were carrying on.<br />
<br />
So he opened his laptop and started his podcast from last weeks service, which he missed again. Oversleeping on Sunday was becoming more common these days. He couldn't find a good enough reason to get up, just so he could go to church and fall asleep in the seat once he was there. Miles remembered so vividly being excited about the Message of Faith. World Overcoming Faith Life Church Podcasts were his lifeline from this reality that struck him in the face every day. Although he had a hard time believing some times there was always this draw that told him that there had to be more than this. Last Sunday's message, which he missed again, was titled: "The Call to Excellence". He laid his head back and lost himself in the message.... When he woke he had missed all but the first five minutes of introduction and he was 3 miles past his stop. At this time of night that meant waiting up to an hour sometimes for a bus going the other direction, or walking the three miles to home. "Just great!" So Miles pulled the cord, and rushed off the bus to begin the long walk back up the street.<br />
<br />
When he got to his door and he reached into the side pocket of his bag to get out his keys he noticed something that made his heart sink and race at the same time. It was already open. Miles found his keys safely at the bottom of the bag, but his iPod was consipicously missing. Whether it had been stolen or simply dropped he didn't know, but it was gone. That was going to cost money to replace. It had just been given as a gift too. With a fitting end to his rough day he slipped in to the house making sure not to wake the family and fell on the couch and went to sleep instantly. He didn't even bother to change.<br />
<br />
DW
